description:
Tests:
1. Round-trip: build a Document with headings, paragraphs, bold/italic, links, list, simple table, footnote → serialize to markdown → deserialize → serialize again → assert identical markdown
2. Diff test: start from pulled markdown, edit (change heading text, add paragraph, change list item) → deserialize edited → reconcile against base → assert batchUpdate requests match expected
Use existing golden file infrastructure where possible.
title: Write round-trip and diff tests for markdown format

description:
Implement deserialization from markdown files to Document API object (no indices set — reindex_document handles those). Key behaviours:
- Parse with mistletoe using custom token extensions
- Reconstruct correct ParagraphElement types for each inline element
- Reconstruct InlineObjectElement for <x-img>, Person for <x-person>, etc.
- Synthesize list IDs deterministically from list type (same as _from_xml.py)
- Parse [^fn.id] footnotes and reconstruct footnote segments
- Parse header.md/footer.md with segment_id frontmatter
- Tables: parse HTML <table> or GFM pipe table
- Append synthetic trailing paragraph to each segment (same as XML path)
title: Implement _from_markdown.py (markdown → Document)

description: Integration test for the markdown workflow: (1) create a new Google Doc, (2) pull-md to get markdown, (3) edit the markdown (make a change), (4) push-md to write back, (5) pull-md again into a fresh folder, (6) compare markdowns — must be identical
title: End-to-end test: create doc, pull-md, edit, push-md, pull-md again, compare markdowns

description: Add mistletoe to extradoc/pyproject.toml. Define the custom token classes for passthrough entities: XImage, XPerson, XCommentRef, XSoftBreak, XPageBreak, XChip. These are mistletoe SpanTokens that match <x-*> HTML-like tags.
title: Add mistletoe dependency and define markdown token models

description:
Epic: Use Google Docs named ranges to annotate special markdown elements (code blocks, callouts, blockquotes) in the document. Named ranges survive content edits (indices auto-update) and give unambiguous semantic meaning beyond visual formatting heuristics.

Naming convention: extradoc:codeblock:python, extradoc:callout:warning, extradoc:blockquote

Design doc: see conversation history / PR description for full design.
title: Named ranges as semantic annotation layer in markdown serde

description:
Build named-range index (startIndex → type:subtype) from documentTab.namedRanges at serialization time.

When encountering a table StructuralElement:
- Check se.startIndex against extradoc:codeblock:<lang> → serialize as fenced code block
- Check against extradoc:callout:<type> → serialize as > [!TYPE]\n> content
- Check against extradoc:blockquote → serialize as > blockquote
- No match → serialize as normal GFM/HTML table

Heuristic fallback (for docs not created by extradoc): single-cell table + monospace font + grey bg → treat as plain code block.

Entry point: document_to_markdown() receives namedRanges from DocumentTab.
title: Pull side: detect code blocks and callouts via named ranges in _to_markdown.py

description:
Parse fenced code blocks (mistletoe CodeFence) → single-cell table:
- Background: #f3f3f3 grey
- No visible borders (transparent/0pt)
- Font: Courier New 10pt
- One paragraph per line

Parse callout syntax > [!TYPE] → single-cell table with type-specific background:
- warning: #fff3cd, info: #d1ecf1, danger: #f8d7da, tip: #d4edda

Parse block quotes > text → single-cell table with left-border accent, named range extradoc:blockquote.

Store the element type (code block lang, callout type) as metadata on the table structure so the annotation pass can create the correct named range name.
title: Push side: parse fenced code blocks and callouts in _from_markdown.py

description:
After reconcile+push applies body changes, run annotation pass:

1. Fetch updated document
2. Walk all single-cell tables matching code block or callout visual signature
3. Check if extradoc:* named range already covers that table's range
4. If not: emit createNamedRange with correct name (type + language hint)

Language hint storage: embed as first line of cell content using a convention (e.g., a zero-width character prefix or a comment paragraph that is stripped during pull-side serialization). Alternative: pass language hint through from _from_markdown.py via a side-channel (e.g., store in the document model's namedRanges field as a pending annotation with a sentinel index).

This closes the round-trip: push → named range created → subsequent pull correctly identifies code blocks.
title: Annotation pass: createNamedRange for code blocks and callouts after push

description:
Extend the reconciler to diff named ranges that use the extradoc:* namespace:

1. Collect extradoc:* named ranges from base and desired docs
2. Emit deleteNamedRange for ranges removed in desired
3. Emit createNamedRange for ranges added in desired — but indices are post-body-change, so this must be a separate batch or use deferred index resolution

Required for: code block language changes (delete old named range + create new), explicit callout type changes.

The annotation pass (extrasuite-hon.3) handles the simpler case of new code blocks. This task handles the reconciler-level diffs for existing ones.
title: Reconciler: emit createNamedRange / deleteNamedRange for extradoc:* annotations

description: Push side: parse backtick inline code to text run with Courier New 10pt. Pull side: detect monospace font runs and wrap in backticks. Also fix _escape_md() which currently strips backticks.
title: Inline code: parse backtick spans and serialize monospace text runs

description: Bug: relative links like [LICENSE](LICENSE) round-trip as [LICENSE](http://LICENSE). Investigate whether the prefix comes from _from_markdown.py, the Google Docs API normalizing on write, or _to_markdown.py. Fix to preserve relative URLs verbatim.
title: Fix relative URLs in markdown links (http:// prefix being added)

description: End-to-end golden file tests: create doc with fenced code blocks, callouts, inline code. pull-md, verify markdown, edit, push-md, pull-md again, diff must be empty. Also unit tests for each new element type.
title: Tests: round-trip markdown with code blocks, callouts, inline code

description: Add pull-md (serialize as markdown) and push-md (alias for push, auto-detects format) to 'extrasuite doc' subcommands. Steps: (1) add format param to DocsClient.pull(), (2) add cmd_doc_pull_md + cmd_doc_push_md handlers in doc.py, (3) register subparsers and dispatch in __init__.py
title: Add doc pull-md and push-md CLI commands

description: Add format='markdown' option to serde.serialize() and serde.deserialize(). Store chosen format in index.xml so diff/push auto-detect. Update DocsClient.pull() to accept --format=markdown flag. document.md replaces document.xml; styles.xml is dropped.
title: Wire markdown format into serde public API and client

description:
Implement serialization from Document API object to markdown files. Key behaviours:
- Paragraphs: NORMAL_TEXT→plain, HEADING_1-6→#-######, TITLE/SUBTITLE→special
- Inline: bold→**, italic→*, strikethrough→~~, underline→<u>, link→[](url)
- Lists: bullet→-, numbered→1., checkbox→- [ ]/- [x]
- Tables: GFM pipe table when all cells default style; HTML <table> when any cell has style/colspan/rowspan
- Passthrough inline: <x-img id=>, <x-person email= name=>, <x-br/>, <x-chip url= title=>
- Footnotes: [^fn.id] inline, definitions collected at end
- Page breaks: <x-pagebreak/>
- Horizontal rule: ---
- Headers/footers: separate header.md/footer.md with YAML frontmatter containing segment_id
title: Implement _to_markdown.py (Document → markdown)

acceptance_criteria:
- _to_markdown.py converts Document → markdown with custom x-* tokens for passthrough entities
- _from_markdown.py converts markdown → Document (no indices; reindex_document handles those)
- HTML tables used when cells have non-default styles or colspan/rowspan
- GFM pipe tables used for simple tables
- Footnotes use actual Google Docs footnote ID as marker [^fn.id]
- Headers/footers serialized to header.md/footer.md with segment_id in YAML frontmatter
- Bold/italic/strikethrough/underline (<u>) and hyperlinks preserved
- List types: bullet→-, numbered→1., checkbox→- [ ]/- [x]
- Passthrough tokens: <x-img>, <x-person>, <x-comment-ref>, <x-br>, <x-chip>
- Round-trip test: markdown→Document→markdown is identical
- Diff test: edited markdown produces accurate batchUpdate requests
description: Add bidirectional markdown serialization/deserialization as an alternative format to XML in extradoc. This enables agents to work with Google Docs in a simpler, more readable format.
title: Implement markdown serde for extradoc
